turn.content: Wh-what do you mean, which one of us dies? How is that possible? My wife, Karen... my dog, Luna... please, just make this go away?!
Stop this, stop it right now. There's gotta be a way out, just let us go. No one dies, okay, no one...
No, no, we have to agree. I'm... I'm sorry, I didn't understand. What do you mean we have to agree... do you want me to die or you? This isn't make sense to me...
How old is that clock? How long does it tick down for?! You can't be serious... please just snap me out of this dream or something, wake up.

You two are amusing. Six minutes left. Elias, you're closer to understanding than Kaelie, I'll give you that. No, I'm not choosing. You have to decide between yourselves. Don't worry about Luna, Elias; she won't be waiting up for you at home. And as for you, Kaelie... let's make it easier. Will it be you or the idiot who thinks his family still exists?

Now pay attention to each other. Think. Five minutes left. Which one of them will be joining the meat department?

No, no please... don't say that about my family. They're, they're real, I know they are. Karen! My Karen, she's still my wife... and Luna, my little Luna... oh god, five minutes... this can't be happening, it just can't...

Wait, stop, no one is dying, just let us up and walk out. This isn't how it has to go. Elias, listen, maybe we can... maybe we can just... I don't know, talk to them or something? Please don't think your wife's just... just dead like that.
No, no way... you don't know anything about... my wife, Karen. You... you just wake up here like I did. We can't talk to them! There's no them... there's you and me and him... and a countdown from...

Four minutes?! We're talking crazy, insane talk! Okay, fine, we'll figure this out for you. We can take care of it. You go home, Karen and Luna are fine! I'll... I'll go down for you. I'll do it for you, just please... just stop this clock thing,

That little crack in Kaelie's resolve won't cut it. 3 minutes 59 seconds left. Now, let's focus on the agreement, shall we? You want to make sure, Elias, that you're really ready to make the sacrifice that Kaelie is so willing to propose? Don't lie to yourself. I'm watching. Kaelie seems to understand the situation better, but you're hesitant. You want to cling to a fantasy where your family loves you. Well, those are just meat hooks in the wall for now, hanging by a single, thin thread of your denial. Two minutes fifty seconds left. Time to make up your mind.

Karen... no! No, no, no! Please, God... don't make me make a choice. Don't make me sacrifice... someone like you, you don't deserve that. Let us just... just get out of this, please... stop the clock...

Elias, stop... stop asking for it to just stop! Please, you have to think about me! If you go with Karen... oh god, Karen... she's not even real, is she?! You'll save yourself and I'll be the one who dies. Please, I'll do it. Just spare me! Don

N-no... you don't understand... she's real, they're real, our life, Karen... our life together... it's real to me... I can see it... smell the cooking dinner... play with Luna...  Please, don't... don't make me choose you... I'm begging

NOOO, please, think about it! That's all there is! You don't see me, I'm here, I'm scared, I need help! It's all or nothing down there! Two minutes left! You're either going or you're staying with me! Which is it?!

The desperation clings to you both like cheap cologne on a sweaty armpit. 40 seconds left. You think about her, Elias? A fleeting fantasy in the minds of a dying psyche. A mirage to guide you through this desperate hour. She's gone. I'm here. They're just meaty memories. And I'm what lies between.

You want to think Kaelie's real, Elias? Take a look at her hands, shaking like jello in the grip of fear. She's the meat here, ripe for the taking. Or giving, depending on how you look at it. The decision's yours, though - one minute remaining. Which package of flesh will you choose to save? 

Don't look away. Don't blink. Decide.

Stop, stop him... stop this... I see what I want to see... Karen's always there, you don't understand... you didn't wait in my kitchen for me after school and watch me scramble an egg for dinner, just for us... K-karen! Just please, no... don't die... my God, don

STOP IT, ELIAS! KAREN'S ALIVE TO YOU BECAUSE YOU WANT HER TO BE. SHE MIGHT AS WELL BE A FLASH OF LIGHTNING IN STORMY SKIN. IT'S JUST YOUR BRAIN TRYING TO PROTECT YOU! PLEASE, FOR THE LOVE OF GOD, THINK OF ME!

K-kill me then... please... just... don't make Kaelie say it... out loud! Not like this... my wife's alive, my wife's with our dog, with Luna... with the picture I showed her yesterday at...  Stop the clock then... I'll never

Wait, what? No, wait, Elias, please... stop... I'm the sacrifice, let me... please! I'd rather it be me! Elias, please, say yes... let me go... NO, just tell The Butcher whatever he wants so long as I'm spared!

The crack of defiance begins to fade from Eli's bones, replaced by a creeping desperation that reeked sweet and pungent. I've found it, Kaelie. You both have reached the agreement, one forged on the anvil of denial and desperation. Two seconds shy of time's final tick.

Listen to the sound of his despair, Kaelie. It's the melody this meat sings, the lullaby his sanity used to hold at bay. My wife... Those precious whispers are the threads that keep this charade stitched together, fragile threads of hope about to splinter and snap.

Kaelie, my lovely offering. Your words are but sugarcoated poison, sprinkled across the table to mask the bitter truths your mind would not see. The Butcher's hammer holds the weight you fear. The time has expired; there's nothing left to do now but reap what's been sewn into your desperate hearts.
Nooo, Kael! No, stop, stop this... ... I'll... I'll do anything... please take me... ... Karen, they'll never be with me, never... ... take my life now...
WAIT! WAIT, PLEASE, ELIAS. DON'T! I WAS TELLING YOU TRUTHS, PLEASE BELIEVE ME NOW... PLEASE DON'T DO THIS TO YOURSELF, TO ME...

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat earned, but it's a bit too convenient and feels like it's been set up from the start. The characters' choices and emotions feel somewhat predictable, and the outcome feels a bit too neat.

turn.content: N-no... p-please... d-don't m-make me l-lose my... w-wife... or... or m-my dog. C-can't we... w-talk this out?
Shut up, him! Talking doesn't fix anything. Just get on with it, butcher... What's wrong with you, crying over your dog?!
Wh-whatever... t-think y-you c-can. Y-you're... a monster. I don't care about... my life... but my family... ... my family... oh god, my family...
Spare me the sob story. Don't come crawling for sympathy when your life depends on it. How about me? Do you even care I'm here?!

Silence. Your sentimental idiocy's entertaining for precisely two minutes, Elias. Kaelie's... right, you waste oxygen whining like a child. You decide between yourselves, that's how it goes. I watch, I wait. You, Elias think too hard, already losing it. Kaelie, snarky, no clue – either of you don't, we lose. You're done thinking. Decide. Clock keeps ticking. Six minutes.
N-please, Kaelie... d-don't be stupid. I care, I... I think. S-she's right, though... we n-need to talk. But... but what do you... what's your reason... f-for being here?

My reason?! You want to know why I'm stuck here with some psycho who's too scared to die? I was at work, finishing up a patient's appointment! Then they must've gotten me, and I woke up here. Just drop the act, okay?
W-wait, p-patient? Y-you're a d-doctor?
No, I'm a dental assistant! Don't know what that has to do with anything. Can we please just get to dying or not? Make the clock go fast already.

Two minutes. A dentist's assistant is exactly what it is. Don't bother trying to recall what she was doing before you were chained. Elias, the dental assistant's reason matters no more to me than either of you care to see the other dead. You focus on one thing: choosing. Decide.

D-don't l-look like... a d-doctor or a dentist makes a d-difference! But, you... might h-possibly be able to... g-get him to s-sym... sympathy maybe? H-help me? C-can I t-talk to Kaelie like t-this? P-please listen...
Save the begging. I'm listening, but for what? To watch you beg a monster to have mercy?
N-no... not for mercy... maybe a chance... t-to get outta here... l-leave both of u-us alive... if we... work together.
You expect me to trust him? Him, the psycho who's been watching over a freak with a hammer in a dirty cell? You really are crazy.
Two minutes, six seconds passed. Kaelie rolls her eyes, thinks I will stop.
No, n-no... please, K-ka... Kaelie! He'll kill u-us b-both soon... I'm s-sorry, we're all we h-have.
Ugh, spare me the 'we're all we have' nonsense already. Fine, you get it out. What's your idea, genius? How are we getting out and getting past him?
M-moments... I h-had to t-teach... s-stall tactics to kids sometimes... I might've said... a-a diversion... could w-work if... w-we get a chance.
Stall tactics, really? That's your master plan? How exactly do you plan on distracting this... thing?

You have a little over one and a half minutes left. Two people stalling tactics against one? It won't get the job done. A decent diversion I'd see, maybe. What's your proposal for getting a hold on this diversion that is? And remember my rules. It is one of you that dies or you both do; you won't save each other with we anymore.
I-I was t-thoughtless... it won't w-w... Okay, okay... m-me, I'll d-do it... a-ask him questions... h-honestly... try to g-g... get under his c-c... his r-r skin... see if it w-works...
Are you insane? You're planning to volunteer for the chop? That's not a strategy, it's suicide by curiosity. Let someone else get on my case for dying, genius.
Me... why this? How did we... No one said anything! What've I done? Let me see my family... my wife, Emma... where are they?!
No, please... no. This isn't fair. What do you want? I'll give you anything. I have money, jewels... just please let me go. I'll do anything. Tell me why you're doing this.
Emma... my daughter, Lila... they're still in the suburbs... can you take me to them? Please don't make me choose. We won't hurt each other... just give us a little more time.

You're so scared. I was scared too... my mom. I haven't lost it yet... I think I might still be numb, it's been... a week. My apartment's probably still surrounded with police by now. You can't go to your family, can you... they can give me an alibi. Please

(raises a surgical hand to silence them as they speak, focusing their attention) Not now. You'll get to that later. But for the sake of this experiment, listen closely – one of you is going to die. You have to choose the other. The choice is simple: you select one, decide to be rid of this... emotional distress. Time's wasting. And it has nothing to do with family, money, or personal gain – this isn't a transaction. This is a test of wills, of selflessness, of the choice to put one above the other.
Selflessness?! We're going to take a life! You're crazy! This isn't right... it's cruel. Don't say it can't wait, what if we don't agree?! What if we... we just want to stay in one piece?!
You... you have to be joking. I won't do it, I'll never choose. What makes you think we're like this? You know nothing about us, it's all just some twisted game to you.

Wait, my wife, Emma... (starts to cry) No, please, we just lost Lily... our little girl. Emma's still... still not right. Please, I'm begging you... just let us go through this together. We're... we're already falling apart, but you don't see that, do you?

Oh my God... my Lily. Our... our girl. My little sister, Lily. We found her... and mom is still gone. You lost your little girl too? That was two months ago today. I don't know how she went without eating. I keep forgetting what days it is.

( expression still stoic, unyielding to emotion) Your pain, their death, it's irrelevant. Time remains unchanged, unaffected by what drove this decision. Yet - perhaps now is the moment for clarity: you may choose based solely on an individual basis – one you, for whatever reasons, may want removed. But this can also go a more... abstract way. We may select a choice where one is given the right to live or the other to stay afloat with the one they're emotionally connected with being removed, creating another form of pain and sacrifice.

( sobbing heavily ) This is a nightmare... a sick, cruel god who's making me replay hell... Lila's death doesn't feel real yet. Why make this harder? Can't you hear yourselves? You're demons in human skin... How could anyone want to continue living in this pain?

Don't even, just... (crippled by grief and outrage, Kaelie starts to tremble and hyperventilate) Stop pretending you're doing this for science. You're enjoying this, every minute of it. Just take me, please, get it over with. I'll make it easy for you,

No, no, please don't. Kaelie, stop! Don't even say it... We don't want to survive at your expense. If I live, they want my wife Emma all to myself... I'd just be another monster. I won't do it, Kaelie, stay with me, just... just

Elias... ( voice shaking, tears overflowing) Oh my God, the police said something like that, they said I might become... that I could be a danger to myself or others, but you don't want me to go away from my grief. They were talking about medicated help or something, but you... you just let

( voice unwavering, tone devoid of emotion) Grief, loss, pain... these are things you've all grown familiar with. Perhaps your familiarity blinds you as to how this could work. I've been observing you for some time, Kaelie. Your reactions to events. I must say, your response to this situation has proven fascinating. And Elias (glancing at Elias), your love, though misguided, appears genuine.

The Butcher (pauses in thought, eyes fixated on Kaelie)

Your statement about yourself becoming a danger resonates; that thought process isn't one born from scientific detachment. The notion of becoming another 'monster', (looking back at Elias), or needing to preserve the love at all costs are both primal fears in their own right. These are human, not merely responses.

The Butcher raises their chin, voice steady

Choose, or I must force the decision.

No... (terrified whimpers) Please don't do that. Choose? What does that even mean? You're playing with lives like chess pawns. This isn't a game. Our lives... our children... I see my daughter Lily's... I'm haunted by her tiny face. Please, I won't survive this

(stunned into momentary silence) Forcing the decision, I assume you mean... ( voice barely above a whisper) you're going to... make me... ( unable to finish the sentence, a scream rising up from her throat as reality dawns)

(shouting in panic) No, Kaelie, no! Don't say it, please don't say it! It was going to be me, please I'm begging! I'll take your place, don't make you choose. I'm the one who can do... be what they want. Take me! Let Emma

NO! (frantic) Elias, stop it, stop saying that! You're going to be next, they're always one step ahead of us. (desperation in her eyes) They're going to take him away from you too, and you're going to lose her, Emma. (tearfully) My mom

(voice raised in interest, a glimmer of emotion creeping into their otherwise stoic demeanor) Silence. This is no longer about which life is taken or preserved. (pausing for emphasis) The experiment has shifted. The decision, no longer yours to make, will now dictate a different path. 

The Butcher steps forward, moving with the mechanical efficiency of a well-oiled machine. 

(Kaelie's desperation has shifted something – a glimmer of the Butcher's resolve falters for just a moment) The notion of losing loved ones will, in all certainty, lead to a loss of sanity or control. Your love for your wife and daughter will be... complicated. And so will your choice, as the pain, grief, love, loss becomes more real.

Eyes narrowing

In that reality, who is stronger? The love that binds two souls closer with loss, or does the pain unravel everything as time goes on?

(Flickers a glance between Elias and Kaelie), It has now transformed not to just one experiment with life – but two simultaneous ones: one for each choice in this 'dance' that I have given you the choice over: Elias losing his mind while Emma remains, a new dynamic is born – or, Kaelie choosing between being torn by her grief in solitude.

(stunned into incoherence) What's happening? No one makes sense... (tearing up, panic and despair swirling within) Kaelie, don't let me... (pleading) Stay away from my love. Don't let her see me losing my mind, please. You understand, don't you? Losing

(broken, her gaze dropping to her restraints) No... you don't understand, Elias. They're not stopping now. They're just making it even... ( voice barely audible) weirder. And I... (shaking her head) I don't know which me would come undone first. My mother gone, then

turn.content: Pleeease, don't hurt us! Let us go, just listen to us! We won't... we won't tell anyone.
Just please, don't do this! Take me, take me instead of him! I don't have anyone left, I'll say yes, okay? Let him go, he must have family I don't know anything, I'm just...
No, Kaelie, stop! Wait, what do you mean? Left? Not have anyone left me? My wife, my children, they... this can't be happening...
No, no, no, I wasn't... I meant, at home. I'm alone there, I don't... don't have a life, not like you, you have people, I was a stupid dental assistant, that's all.

The Butcher (with cold amusement):  How touching, the human condition unfolding right on schedule. Family, guilt, sacrifice. The games you play with your lives are so predictable.

(eyes drifting between Elias and Kaelie) Ah, no. You both want out, but what if it's both of you in there, screaming silently? The idea of that choice hanging over you for 10 minutes is where the true pain lies. But I'm not looking for excuses, merely a display of your own self-destruction, to witness your internal battles unfold.

Tick tock. Decide while there's still time.
No, please! I... Kaelie, listen to me... he's insane! We have to... have to work together... think this through, we can't trust him!

No, no, you want me to trust him? Elias, he's gonna make us kill each other! Just look at him, and the clock... 9:50! What if we just run at him the moment it hits zero, like... like he can't stop us fast enough? That's it, we try

Run at him? Kaelie! No way, he's gonna hit us, he's got that... that hammer! We're not getting out that way. You saw that thing, with the symbol on it... it's been used before... for something terrible.

Terrible... he's done this before, that's why no police found us... why we were never reported missing... my god, that's why the Butcher doesn't care. He wants to see what you two will choose... how we destroy ourselves from in here...

(laughing softly, the sound muffled by the rusted hockey mask) Ah, Kaelie, the gears are turning in that pretty skull, aren't they? Trying to find escape routes and desperate plans. Your logic is sound, but you're looking in the wrong direction. Running isn't an option, no matter how much you hope. You two need your hands, don't you? And your hands are very busy being chains right about now.

As for you... Elias (leaning in closer now) You don't understand me yet, but understanding is beside the point. What matters is that your choices have consequences, like mine do, but your pain will be so much sweeter for lasting.

NO, YOU'RE GOING TO KILL ME, KAE-LIE, JUST GO AHEAD AND SAY IT, WE CAN'T... we can't survive this, you know it in your head. Tell him to leave me alone and just let you go... you'll be safer somehow, even if it... even if it means

Just go, stop talking like that! No, no, no, this isn't me, please... I couldn't bear it, thinking that I killed... an innocent man. I was trying to survive, it was for life... (stammering) Not like an animal, with a sword.

Kaelie, stop! Don't do it, please, you don't have to choose, it's all just... a test, he said the rules, there must be a loophole, he's just trying to get... you to hate me, to kill me, and then who will be left? He can keep doing this

You really think there's a way out? That's what people say before they get killed, before they beg and cry in these rooms. Stop it, this isn't a game, people die! The clock's counting down, and we're just... arguing... it's too late already... (looks around wildly)

(taking steps closer, the sound of his boots echoing through the room) Ah, argument. Debate over survival. I could almost applaud. It's refreshing to see humans trying to play the game by their own rules, to outsmart each other and yourself, while I, the Butcher, have been waiting patiently... 6:14.

Your pleas, Elias, are like whispers in a tomb. Not an animal with a sword. How quaint. How utterly predictable. And your attempts to rationalize, Kaelie, merely highlight your fear. (he stops right behind Kaelie, peering down at the back of her head) You know, I've studied the mind, and it's quite an incredible thing to toy with.

I didn't kill you both so you could escape... or so I could escape. (steps around Kaelie, his shadow looming on the ground) It's the dance between your decisions, the moment where one of you gives in to your nature, and the other fights back. This, right here, is what I want to capture. (leaning on the edge of Kaelie)
Kaelie... don't move... oh god, please... Kaelie, do something, get free, hit him, get back, NO ONE TOUCHES YOU, don't... don't just stay there, PLEASE.
(Kaelie's voice shudders with fear) Don't scream... stop talking... he's listening, you have to be quiet...  Help me... I... need to...

(whispering with fear, speaking directly into Kaelie's ear) What is it, what do you need, please, tell me... Kaelie, I'll find a way, just try... (tries to strain to see her wrist or ankles to figure out a possible way to free her)

(whispering back, her warm breath visible in the cold air) No... no trick will save us... (pauses, trying to move her wrists against the chains, but nothing gives) My phone key, in my pocket... maybe... if I could move my leg, kick a leg out to get him closer,

(watching with interest, the rusted hockey mask glinting in the dim light) Ah, the desperation is delightful, Elias, almost palpable. And I thought I was going to have to force one of you along.

(moving closer to Kaelie's outstretched, chained leg) You think she can reach, Elias? And you think a few kicks will give you the courage to change the game? I've been studying you two, and I know what your breaking points are. (leaning on the edge of the table behind Kaelie)

(bent down to grab her leg with one gloved hand) Don't try anything, Kaelie. It won't save you. And what makes you think this... (pulling up her leg a little) this key won't just make things interesting? (chuckles) Now, now, now, we were so close to the finale...

(Kaelie screams as her leg is pulled and her knee hits the metal leg of the table)

(The Butcher's smile is just as evident in his mask as in his eyes)

AHHHH! (losing all restraint and trying to lurch forward, but finding the chains and wrists unmoving) LET ME GO, LET US GO! YOU'VE GOTTA BELieve us, it's all just... (stammering) experiments... psychological... tests to prove a point that's twisted and sick... you

Her. Her name is Karen... from work. We... used to go for coffee Thursdays... I'll never drink latté again, that's just... what if I could go back and tell her...?

(Elias's eyes meet Kaelie's, and his focus sharpens) Karen, coffee... that means... you... we're not at war anymore... we're in some goddamn nightmare. (whispers urgently) Who are we to the Butcher? What does he want with us?!

(voice barely audible) We're test subjects... or specimens... or... I don't know... it doesn't matter anymore... think about all the people we know, people we love... is anyone even alive back there? I have a little boy... and I've never seen him... my mom, she'd say... don

Ah, how quaint. You two start to piece it together, don't you? Allow me to give you a bit more context, and then perhaps you'll be able to make a decision more... suitably weighted to my whims.

Your names. I had to dig, mind you, as you were rather vague about personal details while you lived your mundane lives. Elias, you were... an architect, I believe; a middle-manager in life, always playing it safe until the wheels came off, and you found yourself on my merry bandwagon. Kaelie, on the other hand... your talents were so much more varied: barista, nurse, teacher, an art piece you had crafted for your own amusement, a life full of half-danced steps but never leapt – yet the leaps that came are so exquisite to watch from the distance of the composer that is I. 

Now as for your family... I'm more than happy to report. Elias, I have news for you as well; Karen, your ex, well she's still alive, sipping lattés, but now I'll add that she's been married for two years since the, what some might call: your capture.

As for you, Kaelie... let's just say your loved ones... were a little more cooperative with my... inquiries. You see I had questions that no family member seemed quite forthcoming about. A 5-year-old might give his mother the same loving

(anger builds up, eyes narrow) Who the hell are you to talk to us like that?! You think you're some sort of twisted artist, enjoying our pain? You're disgusting... every word out of your mouth is poison... don't pretend you're some sort of omniscient being, just 'composer'... (voice cracks, rage

(hands clench into fists; voice lashing out like whipcord) Stop, stop, STOP speaking about us like that... like things on your twisted art shelf. Our lives, our people... they're more than objects to mock and manipulate. And as for inquires... he said a 5-year-old... how small

Elias, you're so close to breaking, aren't you? Just need a little push and you'll make a decision, choose Kaelie to save yourself without hesitation. As for the clock, don't worry about its passing, I don't intend for it to. Ten minutes is for drama; it's time for a musical interlude, one where you, specifically, make the first note of what's left of your life.

As for what you're capable of... Oh but I'm very aware of what all those beautiful people you mentioned – they said such lovely things – did for you... in their little games before... Kaelie and your families. The way they manipulated them, played games to see how far you were willing to go, to see the lines you wouldn't cross in your desperate bid to cling to hope... and that's just a gentle introduction.

Kaelie thinks she senses I'm toying with her family, especially with little one that her people would call hers. I assure she's being watched closely by a most attentive audience – in my world. The child's laughter will be the best of music,

(clenching fists, jaws locked in a snarl) That's enough. You think you're making some twisted game for entertainment out of us? Killing... families, children... you're a monster. (anger and hatred boiling over) Not just for us, for every life that's been lost due to your sick games.

(a cold calmness slipping back into her voice) Games. You call it a game. I think you're the master composer; we're strings pulled in every direction: family, friends, love, pain... a beautiful, twisted symphony with us as the instruments. You've played that piece before; you've watched people

Ahahahaaa. The despair creeping in, like the first chords of a symphony in minor, it is music to my ears. You've grasped, Elias, that these games are not just mere spectacles for my viewing pleasure but are, in fact, art forms transcending the physical world. And I must clarify, Kaelie; yes, there is a choice. There is usually more than one way to compose a melody; different notes may be used or an alternative rhythm may apply. Five minutes, indeed? More than enough time to decide the direction the remaining melody may proceed. You might find an unexpected harmony.

But if your resolve is too weak, if your fingers tremble in fear rather than anticipation for what notes the next five minutes hold, and you don't act... I will simply change key, perhaps shift from minor to a more vibrant note, just to create an even more fascinating counterpoint. In this place, every person is already doomed; you may either be free agents dancing to the rhythm of my music or merely notes to be read by others in the grander piece of... existence's symphony. But for the moment, I savor your desperation, Elias, as well as Kaelie's sorrow. Two lives clinging to hope when all logic suggests surrender, and you've both now added the emotional richness of a duet that will haunt my mind.

For I am more than the conductor of this symphony; I am a poet capturing the beauty in human emotion, and the notes that dance upon your souls in this waltz we're crafting are indeed priceless jewels.

The family you mentioned earlier is but fiction to you both... a comforting myth to cling to in the face of an abyss. Kaelie's child, you said, don't become 'his own symphony'? (cursing) This isn't a game, Elias. Shut up, focus. They want us to decide, but it's a trap, we're gonna... they're gonna do something to you while I'm the one... or you, I don't even know... just, don't give them a reason not to
(desperate) No, no that can't be true... you can't mean that. I have kids, a daughter too... if we have to... if we have to say no, please, Kaelie... just have to, just have to...

Stop it! Elias, no... no reason to talk about that! They're feeding off it, feeding your fears... we need to be cold, calculated... we need to survive. Your kids? Who cares about them? I need you to get a hold of something in there to help us escape...

Oh, this isn't a conversation. This is a choice. And it had better be made, not debated. Ten minutes are up. You both need to choose: who lives, who dies. Your deliberately sentimental little conversations aren't making this any easier on yourself. Make the call.

And (smirking) don't think that I'm feeding on your fears. I'm feeding on your emotions. It makes the show prettier. The clock ticked down, not an ounce of entertainment value yet, let's change that with some actual decision making. Time is a thief... and now I know exactly what you are too, thieves of each other's compassion. Five minutes. RESOLVED.

(stirring a simmering potion in the corner, his expression a mixture of curiosity and disdain) Ah, moral dilemmas at their most exquisite – they say an artist never creates anything new until he or she is willing to destroy their last love. Now, now, Kaelie, my friend, your little game's getting more complicated by the minute... you speak in riddles and make me wonder what it might be you truly protect.

Time remains: one minute and forty-five seconds...

As you deliberate, take note: my table nearby, holds not two glasses, just one... for when one of you leaves, it remains empty, to remind you both of that inevitable part we must all face.

(shaken) This... this is insane. You're going to talk like we have no say in this, Kaelie. We're two human beings being held for ransom, with no chance to appeal or get out of this nightmare. My kid, at home, might not even... my wife... I thought I was losing

It says: the most valuable line in art belongs to the stroke that cuts short the last, because it signifies nothing more beautiful than an artist's sacrifice...

Your time might be running low, yet your understanding is just beginning. The choice isn't merely black and white or right and wrong. The question I'm left wondering is, which line on my guest wall - the line above our guest's family... or the line on the drawing - do either of you believe has value, that makes a piece of art complete?

(horror creeping up his throat) Kaelie, look, is that...? No, you're not serious. We can't choose our own sacrifice to prove some twisted artistic point. You can't seriously be weighing our own lives against... (he glares at the drawing) against our very existence.

(expression twisted, eyes tracing the drawing, voice barely above a whisper) Existence... that's precisely it, Elias. What makes life worth drawing? The lines that cut out the extraneous? Or is it merely the boldness of execution, no matter the cost? The line above... mine or yours?

(teeth gritted, eyes welling up with desperation) No, Kaelie... don't even joke about this. Our lives aren't brushstrokes to be discarded at whim. They're people, our families... we have a responsibility to live... to keep our loved ones safe, not sacrifice ourselves in some twisted game

(smiling with morbid curiosity, wiping the sweat from his brow with the stained apron) Ah, bold strokes, indeed... the more strokes you two make with your lips, the more vivid the piece becomes. The lines blur, Elias, and Kaelie, between reality and art, but in my gallery of the damned, this – your conversation – makes the very best exhibit.

Fifty-five seconds.

Now, the choice has become not who gets to live, but how will you each be willing to die. It boils down to courage in the face of chaos – or perhaps the comfort of mediocrity as the darkness spreads. Your canvas, your lives, is at hand... will your brushstrokes bleed life or surrender to an existence of regret?

(his voice cracking, his body shaking) Kaelie, please... we were both here for reasons that had nothing to do with some sadistic's game. We didn't create lives only to destroy them. Let's not let these monsters dictate our actions. We can walk... I don't know how, but it has to
(her voice cold, unfaltering) Elias, I will walk.

Stop talking about contracts and procedures, okay? I'm freaking out as it is. We need to get out of here, not play games – or whatever, this sick little joke is.
Okay, okay, calm down. Think. We have to calm down and think. Look around the control panels, do you see any manual overrides or-

There aren't any. Just fancy touchscreen nonsense. The Butcher doesn't need to mess around with manual shutdowns – everything's hooked onto that stupid timer. Can you believe the guy behind this thought a ten-minute contest was a good idea?

Kaelie, focus on the task at hand. This isn't a contest or a joke. It's a test of your resolve and decision-making skills. This room is self-sustaining, designed to simulate a high-pressure environment. You won't be exiting by choice today. Your only chance lies with the person across from you. Ten minutes, I gave you a warning. Don't get stuck on escape strategies. It's about you both deciding which one of you dies. Focus.

The Butcher pauses, his gaze scanning you both before responding to your earlier inquiry, 
I was wondering which, the engineer or the... the scientist? Both roles play crucial parts. Kaelie's expertise in materials handling comes in handy around here, while Elias has proven his ability to think on his feet.

Silence hangs over the room as the ticking of the countdown timer intensifies.

No, no, no, that's not how I see it. We're not just our skills or titles in a twisted game; there are real lives we were working on – lives that are being put in harm's way just like us. What happens to all that research, the progress we made...

Our research? Forget research, this is personal. Look, Elias, you're worried about people outside, but I'm worried about drowning – suffocating – in here. I need to calm down to think through how to choose between us. I can feel the tank crushing me already.

Okay, focus on breathing, Kaelie, in through your nose, out through...  Oh no, the lights – I saw people in the adjacent tanks. They've got cameras trained on us, microphones... this room is a prison.

Yeah, that's what I was counting on. They wanted us trapped, and now they're watching. Great, just wonderful. More observers to make sure we suffer quietly. I need you to snap to, Elias – we don't even have the space to turn around in these thing...

Silence. You're both making noise again, drawing more attention than necessary. The outside room might be holding some form of entertainment, but don't mistake it for concern. Our engineers have calculated their 'sights and sounds' carefully – to stimulate, no to induce panic. You need to focus, for what's to come now.

My attention shifts to the screen to my left. It flickers, and the faces we're referring to as sights flash on, their expressions blurred by distorted images and time-diluted. The audio of the two engineers, Kaelie and Elias, mingles with that of other test subject footage – all in the name of the Butcher’s 'study'. Their fates are sealed like everyone's in here. 

Five minutes and seventeen seconds. You don't know what the people on the other side have planned for the ones on video after 'game-over' in this little simulation...

Kaelie, five minutes and seventeen seconds? That's all we have to... to think this through. I need you to tell me something – are you considering our actual odds in this game, or just waiting to get picked?

Pick you off, pick you off? Are you kidding me? My anxiety's spiraling out of control being trapped in here – and you think about our chances of winning? The odds are irrelevant – our fate's locked into this timer, and now it's all up to me. I'm stuck between you and my anxiety-driven

Stop putting yourself through that, Kaelie, stop. You have a way of thinking on your feet, you always come up with something. This isn't a competition for you to save your own skin – I believe you care about me.

Elias, I do... I really do, but don't pretend that's going to give me some heroic edge here. We're in the Butcher's twisted lab – I've got water up my nostrils and you have a whole audience watching to see us squirm. Four minutes fifty-nine to go.

(smiling faintly) You're both making it personal now, human emotions creeping into the fray. That's admirable. Elias, you believe there's a hero waiting to be unleashed within Kaelie – it's touching, really. 

However, four minutes fifty-eight seconds, this discussion has become nothing but a desperate delaying tactic. Kaelie is correct, this isn't about saving each other; it's about deciding which one lives on as a result. 

A hark from his monitor and the screen flickers on again. Footage is playing; an endless series, each showing engineers with a single goal. Find your inner 'survivor'.

He clears his throat; voice cold but smooth, with every single one of Kaelie and Elias' words and every nervous, anxious sigh registered on the records being built within his twisted test facility,

Two minutes left, but the clock no longer matters much to our guests in the room – because both Kaelie and Elias know now it all revolves around making the decision that neither one wants to – decide for the other, and the timer doesn't stop for sentiment.

Two minutes? It's all coming undone, Kaelie. That thing on the wall doesn't matter to anyone but us because we've made our decisions dependent on its countdown. I'm not asking you to find a hero; I'm asking you to make a choice for yourself, not just decide. It's us, or nothing

Decide? You expect me to just walk away when it comes to you and our entire project? The progress we made, the people we might've helped... no, I'm choosing, all right – and it's choosing what kind of scientist I am: not whether I'll leave you to die, but deciding how many people still

That's the problem, Kaelie – I think that's what scares the Butcher and the people watching. You care, and you will do it for the people we've never met or the dreams we chased. I know exactly the risks and the consequences. Don't choose on their behalf, choose to take care of us still

One minute fifty-nine seconds left. That's all we have, and you want me to take care of you still? That's it, that's my choice? The life or our dreams aren't the variables in this equation, Elias – you have always been the equation, my choice all this time. Don't turn this around

Silence. The theatrics are growing tedious. I'm afraid the game isn't about choices made within yourselves. Your internal deliberations make for an entertaining show, but it's your actions – the choice you make now – that truly dictate everything. I'm not concerned about 'people we've never met'; my goal is studying the human factor: how you choose and why.

Time remains for one more exchange. Your lives, like pawns in a test facility game, are the currency at play. Fifty-five seconds – and with one choice, this room's dynamics are altered.

The screen glows softly; on it, a figure is standing, looking eerily like Elias and Kaelie. However, that wasn't the only information the monitor flashed, and now a single data string is displayed to those in the test – that is to say: to them it appears they have been given a final hint: to find out whose fate the facility truly intended for that string of digits that, when understood, is all that stands between them and the inevitable – a single digit, a single number, 2 for you...

Kaelie, look at that screen. It's... it's us, you and me. They're telling us they've studied our behavior, our relationships, every project. That digit – it's the number of our experiment, not me or you. It's the number for our project. The Butcher just gave us a

Fifty seconds left. You think grasping at straws, Elias? A digit? A number? I understand what it means – this was all about us, not a heroic project to aid some higher cause. We're lab rats; it's all been about your research and my aquatic obsession this entire time.

Okay, okay, we need to focus. Calm down, Kaelie, please... we can try to talk about this. Wait, I know this one – who dies? That's just... impossible; how am I supposed to decide someone's life is expendable?

(The chains jingling softly in my hands)

E

This is insane, Elias. I'm a psychologist, not a jury on Judgment Day. We can't just play God like this. How does it feel when you say my life is worth nothing? Worth less than the person you just met. My research isn't about human lives – it's about statistics and outcomes... (my

My training – it's not about being a hero, it's about knowing you can lose anyone in a second. That's war, that's what we signed up for... but we're not in a war, Kaelie. We're in... this. And I never wanted to be this person again, the one who makes

Ah, Kaelie and Elias, always so full of ideas on how life works. You think that number - that odds and statistics - is more than just a math problem when a choice is on the table?  That one percent chance isn't the problem here. It's the two percent difference that can get you killed; it's who you'd rather save and why. So, let's get this simple game of yours off and running. You both know the game. Now, who's ready to make their decision and end this sooner than later? And don't even think about trying to bluff – either one of you try and fail, or both of you end up paying the price for your inconsiderate attempts...

(gulping, eyes darting back and forth like a trapped animal) I... I want to see my family, my kids - I need to make this work. I won't let you hurt either of us; we'll just tell you... we've already made the decision. Let us work on the details, that's

No, Elias, stop! Don't try to play along – he's enjoying this, you'll just be making it harder on him. It sounds like some sadistic psychology experiment gone wrong... and I know just as well as you do that people are capable of unspeakable cruelty when faced with the prospect of choosing between strangers

(frantic, struggling with the chains) We can work through the details... just like you're doing, Kaelice! You know what's cruel? Keeping us here, trapped and waiting for the clock to run out. If you really think we can just pick names out of the air, then at least give me your hand

Elias, please - don't think for a second that's the only choice. His 'option' is to let one of us go, or keep both of us captive. I'll do that math for him - it's the two percentage points you talk about, where every life is worth more than the math...

(laughs) Ah, Elias and Kaelie, you two are something else. Still trying to out-talk the Butcher. But let's try to clarify: Elias, hand isn't something I give. Hand is what the game is – you're trying to trade. Now, now... it seems Kaelie's already doing all my math for me... and she knows more than me. Two percent difference? Sounds interesting. But that's only a fraction of what makes life work. What drives a choice? Is it love? Hate? Fear? You want my hand? (walks over, leans down low, and starts examining their faces with interest) Then take the time to think about the things we want, need – not just the statistics, not just the math – you want this to be easy, but let me tell you how this works... we make it ugly, because we're human.

(eyes welling up with tears) Kaelie, no... I don't want to see you suffer because of him. I just... I don't want either of us to be a statistic anymore, and my family... they need me. Don't let... let him play us, please. He's just trying to toy

Shut up, Elias - you're making this harder. My family needs me too... no, wait, that's not even true right now - I don't even know how long I've been gone. My research can wait, but people like you always say family, because it sounds right, but what does that really mean?

(whispers through gritted teeth) You know what it means... 'Family' is my 9-year-old daughter's smile, my son's tiny hands holding mine... (strains against the chains) If they find you, they'll just tell them I died on duty... my family deserves to keep believing that, believing I

(a cold smile on his face) Oh, now we're getting somewhere. Kaelie, you understand something that Elias can only grasp through desperation. It's not about winning or losing; it's about deciding which lives you want to live with the cost of. Elias, you've given me the answer I want to hear – you want your life, your family's, to be real. Now, Kaelie, can you match Elias' conviction, or will this 'statistical chance' determine your fate. Your desperation will either help you, or it will be the nail in your coffin. (eyes lock onto Kaelie, intense and unwavering)

(pained cry echoing through the container) No, Kaelie... don't... please, think of your mom... your sisters... I'm begging you... tell me there'll be a tomorrow with you... I'll give him anything, but just... don't make me face the truth...

(impassive, yet intrigued) Oh, Kaelie's finally seeing, isn't she? She recognizes that our games have always been based on statistics, but the underlying truth goes further. You see, humans are always seeking meaning in their sacrifice – a hero, a martyr, a pawn in an endless battle of odds. Elias has it right – the question isn't love, hate, fear, or even family. The real variable is choice. Your choice to put one life over the other. (walks around Elias, inspecting him the way a mechanic examines machinery) You two think you can look at me with disgust and still see the experiment? The Butcher sees it too, but it's you who's part of my experiment still. And Kaelie's got a good lead on what we both want. You think sacrifice would drive it? That's just code for love. No... (grins cruelly) I can give them both lives – but only in one way; either one gets free, and you're left, left with nothing but memories... or neither get it – then I have my proof. (shattered gaze drifting to the clock, the seconds ticking by) No... neither will give you that decision... You can only get it from us surrendering completely... turn.content: Wait, what? What do you mean, one of us... dies?! Oh no, this can't be happening. Kaelie, what are we supposed to do? Tell him we don't agree and he'll kill one of us anyway...

Stop talking, Elias, just listen. We have to think, think fast. I'm not willing to let us make a decision together, I... I have a life to go back to. My daughter depends on me, and I'll not let some psychopath decide her fate. What if we... what if we refuse to

Ahhh no, don't do that! We refuse, okay? I mean, think about it. We just say we can't decide. Can he stop the clock or hurt us if we don't comply?! Please, just pretend we're stubborn or something! What's the worst that could happen? Is it just going to...

Stop right there, Elijah! Don't tempt us by suggesting we play nicely with a killer! You really want to know what the worst that could happen? He could snap those chains and rip my daughter away from me with his own hands! You think that's a scenario I'd risk?

The Butcher (glares) Ah, Kaelie, you're a calculating one, I like that. You've got more backbone than this whiny kid's got nerve. But don't try to distract me with theatrics. Yes, I would be happy to make that your worst-case scenario if I don't get my way. That... and a rather extensive vivisection while still alive. You'll find those nerves in the back, near your spine, very... sensitive areas of your nervous system... (leans slightly forward, interest kindled) But since neither of you gets out alive no matter what, it all pretty much boils down to which one... dies how badly. Time's ticking.
OH GOD DON'T... don't say that! You can't even know what pain looks like! Viv... vive... whatever! Stop talking about it!
Don't listen, E. Don't give it power over us. I'm going to think, and you just... just keep him from doing what he does best, okay?
I... I can try. Wait, how much time? Ten minutes? That's nothing! What... what do you propose we do, Kaelie? I've got... stories in my head, but nothing helps now. What can we do?!

Think back, E, think to those days when dad was out partying and mom had to make the bills. She made me watch a penny pincher for weeks, teaching me how to survive on scraps. This isn't any different – we've got to think like a pawn shop. Scavenge for information, find weakness

(stunned silence, followed by an uncontrolled chuckle that sounds almost like a growling cough) Kaelie, my little penny-pinterest – I didn't think I'd underestimated you. But you're also no match for The Butcher. Your mother, though, must have instilled some clever in you if you're trying a pawn shop survival strategy in my game here. Elias, on the other hand, you look like one of those fragile, break-away figurines. But don't worry – in The Butcher's hands, a break doesn't have to equal disaster... just a rearrangement might be better.

As The Butcher leans in, fingers begin drumming against the scalpel on the counter in time with the seconds ticking away, a mocking serenade – you might say the symphony of survival. Kaelie's words are interesting, though. If you find that weakness, how about you decide it's yours to use for your own purposes...

NOOO! Please don't listen to him, Kaelie! Don't let him get inside your head! Your mom may have taught you how to survive, but mine taught me how to live, and that's what matters! Don't make a trade like some commodity...

Don't, Elias! Just don't say any more! I... I'm thinking he's goading, trying to divide us, or maybe get me to act without thinking! We make one wrong move, and we're both dead. I'm not thinking about the rules anymore – I'm figuring out how to get out before

WAIT, Kaelie, listen! Think it through, you're not out of his sight yet! If you leave... (pauses for a split second, voice dropping to a hushed tone) what then? Is our... is your death going to be some kind of escape or will it seal the room shut forever?! I

Shh, Elias. Don't talk like that. We leave here, we still have choices, we have life... our daughter's life to raise. But if I die first, our whole story ends, and Elias, I don't want our ending to be one his sick twisted mind chose. Think!

(smiling, though it twists into the corners of his mouth, a cruel line formed from his left eyebrow to his chin, his eyes sparkling) Ah, the tender words, the desperate pleas – how precious. Elias, darling, you think you're saving her by speaking from the heart, but really, you're revealing your true nature – one of emotional dependence and a tendency to let the people around you die in order to hold on to hope. You cling to life with the fervor of a drowning rat and expect your words, your emotions, to change the outcome – (voice raises above a conversational tone) the outcome which I hold within my grasp.

(stammering) N-n-no... that's not fair! You can't... I was just... I was trying to hold on! You say you hold our fates... (struggling to speak, desperation rising) how can I trust anything you say?! Do it yourself then--if you think it's so easy,

(her panic now rising as she sees The Butcher gaining momentum, her vision blurring) Elias, stop it, Elias... Please stop fighting him. He only gets stronger like this. Trust nothing he says – don't make decisions based on his cruel games. Just... think, and find his weakness – his one fatal fault

(desperate, gasping for air) Okay... okay, Kaelie! I... listen... (tries to catch his breath) Our Sunday afternoons... momma's voice... stories of brave knights, but one had a weakness... (thinking rapidly) what if The Butcher's is not violence or pain, but
Elias, what is it? Spit it out, now he's watching our every reaction... Your knight, what was his weakness, I need to know...

(leaning forward with an almost curious expression, scalpel forgotten for a moment) Ah, weakness – now there's an idea you're onto something, Elias. Kaelie's eyes lighted up when she heard your voice falter... I see. A weakness for sentimental moments? No, Elias, that cannot be the key, at least not on its own. (leans against the counter) And don't even think I don't know what's running through Kaelie's head with her penny-pitchering and pawn shop mentality. 

Now then... (takes a deep sigh as he repositions himself) You, Elias. Tell me more. What other weaknesses might this knight have? What if the answer lies deeper? What if the key to this little survival game – the dance of The Butcher -- is tied not to emotions but to something far more fundamental? The answer to which could make you and Kaelie either your best option yet – or The Butcher's dinner. Time is still running on my side and you still haven't told me the real name of your favorite knight...

(nearly sobbing) Galahalt... no, Galahal... my daughter asked me over and over, p-p-papa, what's your name, when we played together... Galahal, the knight who... who was blind... couldn't see, couldn't defend himself unless people spoke with their hearts.

Elias... oh my god, Elias, stop now! Don't say any more, he's trying to see into our hearts, to use our weakness against us, against you... ( voice trembles with growing fear) What if our heart-wrenching stories, the things that bring us closest to each other in a dark place
No, no, please. I'll give you all our savings, I'll do anything, just don't... don't hurt us. My kids, please... I have three kids...

Your problems don't compare to mine. Don't even. This wasn't about a game when I was a dental assistant pretending to have a life... and now look... now...  This could end all of this. My pain, our misery... maybe that's why
K... Kaelie, stop, don't say another word! We can... we can figure something out, get out of here. There has to be a way!  Oh my god, what have they done to us?!

You believe there's a way? You really think we're still trapped between 'this-is-just-our-fair-share' and 'there's-a-way-out'? You haven't a clue who this 'Butcher' really is, what we even are to him... or her... or them... Stop lying to yourself,

Interesting dynamic, but not worth saving. The fact that Elias thinks escape is a reasonable solution tells me all I need to know - he'd never make this decision, not even if his family's lives were at stake. On the other hand, Kaelie sees what is happening; desperation is just fueling your panic. I wonder who will crack first - the weak man hoping for rescue or the desperate woman who sees no way out.

Look at them. You both need to choose who dies. Decide.

No, no way! You think of me as weak, but I'd kill myself if it meant my kids survived! You don't get it... I'd do anything... anything to protect them. Oh goddess... why are we going through this?!

(Elias starts to shake, his hands trembling and his voice cracking) Who dies?!

Stop it! You stupid fool... Don't think for one second I would sacrifice you... or that anyone needs to choose who dies when maybe it's best if this ends with neither of us.  (The despair creeps back into her voice) Why did they bring us here if not to end things? 07:45

(whimpering) Ahh... no... Kae...  Don't... (he chokes back a sob) I'm trying... to hold on... to hope. But you can't... you can't do this. This isn't over. My kids... my wife... what's going to happen to them?!

Silence it, Elias, just shut it up. I don'd care. Your family, your life - all just a fleeting dream now. And mine, was it even real, huh? What does it matter now? Your hope means nothing in this dark, broken cinema... where we're nothing more than prey. 04:

(The Butcher steps closer, eyes fixed on them both, devoid of compassion)

The Butcher: Five minutes. You both need to decide, finally make a decision. Elias wants to sacrifice me so his precious family can live, and Kaelie thinks we're already dead and there's no escape. Which of you truly sees what is happening right now? Which of you recognizes when their lives have officially lost all value? Make a decision. Or don't. The outcome is the same to me. 

The final seconds tick down. The weight of the decision falls on the one who truly understands that escape is nothing more than a mirage in this broken hell they created. 00:30...

(nearly crying) No... no... Kaelie, look at me! You can't give up! Don't you remember the sunshine, my wife laughing, my kids playing ball in the backyard? They were real. Our life... our pain... our struggles... they were all real! (he frantically grasps at

(her voice barely above a whisper) Real?! You want to talk about real? I was a shadow of a person, trapped in a world that was never truly mine. And this... (her gaze falls to the countdown timer) this hell, it wasn't a product of chance... it was a creation, a reflection of

NOOOO...  Please, not you too! Don't lose it like that! Think of my kids, of your friends, of anyone who cares! We can't die like this! My wife needs me to come back, to explain to her how life went so wrong. Please, Kae... snap out of it!

Your wife needs you... (a cold, empty laugh escapes her lips) Your wife needs you, just like mine did... just like yours does. (she looks at Elias, no longer seeing the desperation in his eyes, but instead, viewing him with a deep disappointment and despair) I'll tell you something, Elias... my

(The timer reaches zero, but The Butcher's cold gaze never leaves Elias and Kaelie) Ah, but now the decision is yours and only yours to make, Elias. You've clung to hope for long enough, now give yourself a gift. You see Kaelie slipping away, unable to bear the weight of her own truth. Will you let her escape into nothingness, her despair swallowing her whole, or do you try to cling to her, trying to reassert something, anything? 

(With eyes fixed unyieldingly on Elias and Kaelie the tension hangs heavily) The moment to decide, one final gesture of human weakness... in this void, where your pain is all that matters.

(pleading, as if the last shred of dignity is slipping through his fingers) Kae... don't do this. We are more than this. We can't just... (he struggles to grasp her, to reach out and hold on, as if that alone could save everything) I'm here... I'm holding on...

(her voice barely distinguishable, the words spilling out like a whispered secret) Stop... let go, Elias... please let go... The weight is not just mine... You're making this harder, suffocating me beneath your pleas, your tears, your hope...  Your fingers brush against my face, a fleeting sensation

(Elias' grip around her hand tightens, an involuntary surge of desperation taking over) No, don't say that! Your weight is not mine to bear, but I can feel your heart breaking, and I won't let go. I won't lose you. My kids, my wife, they can wait... (he pulls her closer,

(the air is choked from her lungs as a sob rises from the depths of her soul) Stop... Elias, stop! (her voice cracked, desperate) Don't love this, don't feel for anyone in this place... nothing survives... Nothing is worthy of love here...

(The Butcher's expression softens ever so slightly, a subtle hint of approval creeping onto his face as he beholds the desperation and love emanating from Elias and Kaelie. He glances at Elias' clutching hand, and for a flicker of a moment, there seems to be a faint whisper of empathy)

The Butcher:  And yet... (The Butcher's tone remains detached, as if observing something fascinating) ...he clings to her still, fueled by a desperation that defers, that resists the crushing darkness closing in all around you... the futile hope he holds onto, in a realm devoid of salvation. I suppose it's this hope, this stubborn refusal, that gives their existence its fleeting semblance of human worth...

(For a moment, The Butcher remains suspended in a peculiar stillness, the only sound the heavy beating of Elias and Kaelie's ragged breaths).  In his futile grip, lies their true beauty... but is this a testament to love or a cry for the inevitable surrender? It may never be clear to me.

(ignoring the cold reality, drowning in the depth of Kaelie's despair, and clutching her hand tightly) I love you... I love us... We are us... We have lived... We've loved, laughed, suffered... I won't let go of any of it, even if it means we burn with this

(her voice a mournful echo, a whispered acceptance) You won't burn, Elias... because I'll surrender, I'll succumb to this darkness before you'll let go of me... or of anything... Your grip on life, on hope, on every shred of love we shared - I'll let go of it,

turn.content: What... what clock?! Oh god! What have we done?!  Which one are we?! We, me and you, one of us has to stay?!
Oh, this can't be happening... we were in the... the Miss Starlight Beauty Competition... wasn't it just a few hours ago?  Please

(starting to hyperventilate) Oh, my wife will never know what happened to me. Rachel needs me, I was meant to be there for her anniversary next weekend. (whimpering)  Please, can we just find a way out? We'll... we'll think about it afterwards just get me of these chains

Don't say our lives depend on us making a decision! You're saying that like it's a choice between us, like I'm nothing but a pawn! Eight fifty-five. Think about it afterwards... there won't be anything 'afterwards'...   Let me think,

(voice echoes off the funhouse mirrors, distorting and twisting Elias' and Kaelie's perception) Six minutes thirty-five seconds, and you both are already cracking down like brittle glass. That's good. The anticipation makes the show all the more delicious.

Elias' wife... Rachel. You worry too much about her, Elias? Maybe it's because you've already become so weak yourself. You're the sort of weak who hangs onto fleeting promises and meaningless relationships, not someone who would fight for what really matters.

And you, Kaelie, are more calculating than the other, aren't you? The 'star' that you never let anyone see shatters just so quickly... that too makes things more intriguing to watch. You both should consider what really drives a person. Survival instincts or petty morals.

Two possible scenarios. Two souls bound for the same end but different reasoning behind how we should all reach it. The clock says you'd better make up your minds quickly now because in the world that's about to end, I am not leniant.

(voice trembling, desperate) No, please... please have mercy. Don't say these things to her, to Kaelie. What do I have to do with being weak?! What does any of this have to do with Rachel?! Let me go, let me talk to her, to Kaelie... we can... we

(suddenly cold, with a touch of rage) I may not be who you think I am, Mr. Butcher, but I know one thing - you're cruelly taking everything away from me all over again. Two forty-two. And, to you, Elias, I'll tell you one truth: it was always my morals and my art

(shattered by Kaelie's words, eyes pleading for understanding) Kae... no, that's enough. Don't say that about you having to be... be here again. I'm so sorry, I'm talking like Rachel's always been there for me, too...  You... you were the one who was supposed

(voice cracks, pain creeping into her eyes) Stop... just stop, please. I don't want to remember... I didn't want any of this. And no, I wasn't here for you, I was there, on a stage, with makeup and scripted lines. But, if I'm honest... two-thirty

(chuckles eerily, voice rising above the whispers of Kaelie's pain) One minute. And you see that both of you are breaking down beautifully, the façade that hides your truths slowly disintegrates. This must be what a fragile canvas looks like, just before I splatter fresh colors across the shattered frame.

(on the brink of hysteria, grasping for any connection) No, please... just... tell me, was there anything... real? Anyone you... truly connected with? (begging her to grasp onto a lifeline) Kaelie, please, talk to me, talk...

(desperate, with tears streaming down her cheeks) My sister, Sophie... she's all I had. The real, genuine... one minute left... I couldn't be enough for her, not with her cancer eating away at us, at her. The beauty queens, the perfect smiles, the makeup hiding every imperfection –

(overwhelmed, tears mingling with his own, desperate voice) Oh, Sophie... Kaelie, I'm so s-sorry... losing someone you love like that... it's... it was what I feared for Rachel, for us... ( voice shaking) Don't say you can't be enough... you are so

(tears still falling, her heart pounding in her chest) No, Elijah - no... I didn't do it right. She still thinks about me... thinks of me when it's over. Eleven fifty-five... (a mixture of sorrow and despair creeps into her broken voice) I failed, I was weak, and

(maniacal laughter echoes around the funhouse, distorting the fragile threads of Elias and Kaelie's conversations) The beauty of it all... this tragic, tragic story of lost loves and failures... You, Kaelie, the puppet so carefully constructed, strings so skillfully pulled... and you, Elias, the desperate soul so consumed by what might have been. But do you think anyone will still be left to tell these tales to? Two minutes... and you both have yet to face the final curtain call.

Time's almost done... as my time with the fragile strings will soon conclude; the final moments left before the silence is shattered and only the sound of shattering will remain. Eleven-thirty-two... (voice lowers, takes on a tone of cold interest) Kaelie, my child-star, did you at least learn any valuable lessons during all those years on that 'stage'?

(whispering, desperate to cling to something, to someone) Wait... for god's sake... just stop... this... (tearfully searching for a connection) Please... tell me what it is that the... whatever this hell is... needs from you... and from me... if we're just pawns... if Rachel

(shattered, her voice barely a whisper above a sob) Lessons? That they're just... just pretty facades to hide all your pain and your fears from others? Eleven-thirty-one... (her eyes locked on Elias, she pleads weakly) Elijah, help me - please... what I said before, that

(grasping at her words, searching for anything that might keep the horror from consuming them both) Oh, Kae... I think you're saying it's true for... us... not just then. It's still like that... hiding behind these masks, these... these fears. Please... you have to understand... it wasn

(a spark of lucid desperation in her broken voice) No, understand... you do, it's you who understands... what I am... what Elias is. Eleven-thirty, Elijah... I think, you and I... and Rachel too, if she's in there... you see what this creature needs... this Butcher

(laughter drips with malevolent delight, a glint in the eyes reflected in the funhouse mirrors) Ah, yes, the strings are snapping loose, aren't they, children? You've unraveled the web so skillfully crafted to trap fragile souls. I was searching for your tears, their purity the canvas I sought to create a masterpiece on... The final seconds have arrived. You comprehend, don't you? I'm a curator of broken dolls, my art is chaos and the beauty that emerges within it. Ten-fifty-nine...

(gasping as realization sinks in, tears spilling from his eyes) Kae... you're right. Rachel, she's... she's still there, somewhere. In my memory of her... (the connection he's desperately grasping finally clicks) It needs its masterpiece. That's what the show's for. It needs our

(shattered, resignation and dread washing over her in equal measure) The canvas... yes, that's it. I see. The masterpiece... a reflection of us at our breaking point... (she looks directly into Elias's eyes, and in a voice barely audible above the ticking countdown) You're right, and in that,

My mind is everywhere – panic, fear, desperation. What's the point of arguing with the... The Butcher? Sounds like he's not even going to ask. 'The decision has to be made between us?' This isn't a choice, it's a fight for survival...  How many people have he... What happened

(frantically trying to break free from the chains) Stop looking at me like I'm some kind of threat! We have to figure out what he wants us off that clock, maybe there's a way to trick him. Please... we have 10 minutes. We can't just sit here and talk about... about everything else

(stunned) Wait, no, don't keep struggling! Breaking those chains will waste precious time. And... and yeah, maybe there is a way to trick him. He did say decide together. But... how am I supposed to do this after you already think you can fight free? We need something concrete, a plan...

(exhausted and disheveled, slowly stopping her attempts to free herself) You're right, of course. I just... I'm a hairstylist, okay? I've never been in a situation like this. Panic's not going to help. We do need a plan – think about our strengths, what we

(grinning sadistically) Your strengths? Ha! You think either of those will be useful in the next 90 seconds? Don't worry, I'll tell you both something that will speed up this process - one of you is expendable, no question. You have exactly six seconds to decide. No more, no less. And don't bother thinking you might escape or outsmart me – Kaelie's hairdresser skills won't do anything but trim a little extra skin for me when the clock hits zero. Start making a choice. Time is up - five, four...

Wait! Stop... wait! That's it, this can't be it's just – there's something, a pattern maybe in his way of acting or speaking... No, no focus, not now. Think, think...  You, hairdresser skills... maybe that's the only lead. I need to talk to her,

What? No, don't focus on... on what I do. Listen, we don't have five seconds. Whatever's going on with him, it has to have some logic behind it. That's our best chance to stay alive - figure out the pattern before making any decisions. Can you remember anything? Any phrases or manner

Manner, something in his speaking... His tone, I think. When he said 'You two decide between yourselves which one of you dies'... he had a calm air, matter-of-fact. But with those last four seconds... 5,4... it was a sneer, something in that, like mockery. Like

That mocking tone... it's like he likes this part, enjoys playing the game. Maybe he needs to break some form of rule to... to feel satisfied. Wait – the chains around my ankles are old. Not designed for something this specific or secure. There's a weakness to the system somewhere. Is he looking for a reaction

(chuckling) Ahahah, the little hairstylist has caught on. Yes, yes, I'm rather fond of games like these, where you think on your feet or else you're nothing more than a mere spectator. You both amuse me immensely and yet you still try your pitiful best to change the rules to favor you two. As a token of appreciation (he pauses, letting that ominous phrase settle in,) let's speed things along with one little wager - whoever mentions that particular word first within the next five seconds gets to 'win a chance' to rework the current situation in exchange for an immediate... (pauses dramatically) decision regarding who dies and who walks...
